_comment: Under CoALA, each MCP server is an additional GROUNDING action surface
(§4.2). Adding a server extends the external action space; removing one
shrinks it. Treat additions as architectural changes, not config tweaks.
Secrets come from Railway environment variables — never hard-code tokens
here. The ${VAR} syntax is expanded at load time.
Servers below are commented out by default; uncomment and configure the
ones you actually need. Each comes with a note on which CoALA grounding

_github_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. Repo browsing, issue/PR ops, code search.

_filesystem_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. Scoped filesystem access. Built-in tools already cover most of this; enable only if you need explicit MCP-level scoping for subagents.

_postgres_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. Read-only DB introspection. Mark production DBs as read-only in URL; writes require explicit promotion.

_railway_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. If a community Railway MCP is available, mount it here. Otherwise the agent uses the railway CLI via the shell tool.

_brave_search_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. External web search beyond the built-in web tools.

_sentry_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. Error/incident telemetry. Essential for the debug-incident skill if you use Sentry.

_disabled_servers_are_ignored: Hermes ignores entries whose key starts with underscore. Rename _github_example → github (and drop _disabled / _note) to activate.
